info.description: One question for the Nervous System Decoder quiz at /free/nervous-system-decoder/quiz. Order matters — the first answer becomes A, second B, third C. Scoring counts A/B/C across all 5 questions (A=Signal Clear, B=Scrambled, C=Faint). You should keep this structure: each question has exactly 3 answers, one per state, in A→B→C order.

text.description: The question itself. Anna's style: short, ends with an ellipsis (…) so the answer completes the sentence. E.g. 'When I try to rest, lately…'

answer_a.description: Answer A — the regulated/grounded response. Counted toward Signal Clear when picked.

answer_b.description: Answer B — the activated/wired response. Counted toward Signal Scrambled.

answer_c.description: Answer C — the flat/shutdown response. Counted toward Signal Faint.
